2605.22137.title: Cross-Lingual Consensus: Aligning Multilingual Cultural Knowledge via Multilingual Self-Consistency
2605.22137.paper_summary: Although Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate strong capabilities across various tasks, they exhibit significant performance discrepancies across languages. While prompting LLMs in English typically yields the highest general performance, it often induces a Western-centric bias, hindering the model's ability to accurately reflect diverse cultural knowledge. We hypothesize that LLMs already possess rich cultural knowledge embedded within local-language representations, but fail to retrieve it when prompted in English. To bridge this cross-lingual knowledge gap, we propose a novel self-supervised framework. Our method leverages multilingual self-consistency to identify the most reliable cultural responses across languages, combined with a self-critique mechanism to transfer this knowledge to the weaker language. Evaluations on the BLEnD benchmark demonstrate that our approach significantly improves cultural alignment-boosting performance on English queries by an average of 5.03%-relying entirely on self-generated data. Ultimately, our work demonstrates that latent cultural knowledge can be successfully surfaced and propagated across languages, enabling more culturally equitable and consistent LLMs.
2605.22137.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在各種任務中展現出強大的能力，但它們在不同語言之間的性能差異顯著。雖然用英語提示LLMs通常會產生最高的一般性能，但這往往會引發西方中心主義的偏見，妨礙模型準確反映多元文化知識的能力。我們假設LLMs已經在本地語言表徵中擁有豐富的文化知識，但在用英語提示時未能檢索到這些知識。為了彌補這一跨語言知識的差距，我們提出了一個新穎的自我監督框架。我們的方法利用多語言自我一致性來識別不同語言中最可靠的文化回應，並結合自我批評機制將這些知識轉移到較弱的語言上。在BLEnD基準上的評估顯示，我們的方法顯著提高了文化對齊的性能，對英語查詢的提升平均達到5.03%，完全依賴自生成的數據。最終，我們的工作表明潛在的文化知識可以成功被挖掘並在不同語言之間傳播，使大型語言模型更加文化公平和一致。
2605.22137.author: Andrew Ivan Soegeng et.al.
2605.22137.authors: Andrew Ivan Soegeng, Patrick Sutanto, Tan Sang Nguyen
